question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use a fee-on-transfer loan token as market.loanToken under the precondition that the token deducts a fee on every transferFrom, trigger take() so that buyerAssets - sellerAssets is transferred to Midnight but only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets - fee) arrives, causing claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to be credited the full spread while the contract holds less, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual token balance attributable to fees, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where fee claimer cannot withdraw full claimableSettlementFee? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer ERC20 as loanToken, execute take(), assert claimableSettlementFee[token] > midnight.balanceOf(token) - withdrawable.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that sellerAssets is transferred directly from payer to receiver (seller) via safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets), trigger take() so that the seller's position.credit is decreased by units but the receiver only receives sellerAssets - fee tokens, violating the invariant that every credit decrease corresponds to an equal token receipt by the seller, causing scoped impact: direct loss of seller funds? Proof idea: fuzz fee percentage, assert receiver.balanceOf after take < sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that repay() pulls units from payer via safeTransferFrom but only (units - fee) arrives, trigger repay(units) so that position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced by units and marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by units, but the contract only received (units - fee), violating the invariant that withdrawable <= actual loan token balance held by Midnight, causing scoped impact: lenders cannot withdraw their full credit entitlement? Proof idea: repay with fee-on-transfer token, assert withdrawable > midnight.balanceOf(loanToken).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that supplyCollateral() pulls assets from msg.sender via safeTransferFrom but only (assets - fee) arrives, trigger supplyCollateral(assets) so that position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] is set to oldCollateral + assets while the contract holds only (assets - fee), violating the invariant that sum(position.collateral[i]) <= actual collateral token balance held by Midnight, causing scoped impact: borrower appears more collateralized than actual holdings, enabling undercollateralized borrowing? Proof idea: supply collateral with fee-on-transfer token, borrow against inflated collateral, assert health check uses phantom collateral.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that withdraw() sends units to receiver via safeTransfer but only (units - fee) arrives at receiver, trigger withdraw(units) so that position.credit and marketState.withdrawable are both reduced by units but receiver gets less, violating the invariant that a lender's credit redemption yields exactly units loan tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss of lender funds on withdrawal? Proof idea: withdraw with fee-on-transfer loanToken, assert receiver balance delta < units.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that withdrawCollateral() sends assets to receiver via safeTransfer but only (assets - fee) arrives, trigger withdrawCollateral(assets) so that position.collateral[collateralIndex] is reduced by assets and the health check passes, but receiver gets less than assets, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal yields exactly assets tokens to receiver, causing scoped impact: direct loss of borrower collateral? Proof idea: withdrawCollateral with fee-on-transfer collateral token, assert receiver balance delta < assets.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator use a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that liquidate() sends seizedAssets to receiver via safeTransfer but only (seizedAssets - fee) arrives, trigger liquidate() so that position.collateral[collateralIndex] is reduced by seizedAssets and position.debt is reduced by repaidUnits, but receiver gets less collateral than expected, violating the invariant that liquidator receives exactly seizedAssets collateral tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss of liquidator funds? Proof idea: liquidate with fee-on-transfer collateral, assert receiver balance delta < seizedAssets.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that liquidate() pulls repaidUnits from payer via safeTransferFrom but only (repaidUnits - fee) arrives, trigger liquidate() so that position.debt is reduced by repaidUnits and marketState.withdrawable is increased by repaidUnits, but the contract only received (repaidUnits - fee), violating the invariant that withdrawable increase equals actual loan tokens received, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw? Proof idea: liquidate with fee-on-transfer loanToken, assert withdrawable increase > actual tokens received.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that claimContinuousFee() sends amount to receiver via safeTransfer but only (amount - fee) arrives, trigger claimContinuousFee(amount) so that marketState.continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable are all reduced by amount but receiver gets less, violating the invariant that fee claim yields exactly amount tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss of fee claimer funds? Proof idea: claim continuous fee with fee-on-transfer token, assert receiver balance delta < amount.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer use a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that claimSettlementFee() reduces claimableSettlementFee[token] by amount and sends amount via safeTransfer but only (amount - fee) arrives, trigger claimSettlementFee(amount, receiver) so that accounting shows zero remaining fees but receiver got less, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee reduction equals tokens received by claimer, causing scoped impact: direct loss of protocol fee revenue? Proof idea: claim settlement fee with fee-on-transfer token, assert receiver balance delta < amount.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (payer path)] Can an unprivileged taker use a rebasing (positive rebase) loan token under the precondition that the token's balances increase between take() calls, trigger multiple take() calls so that marketState.totalUnits and position.credit grow with each take but the actual token balance grows independently via rebase, violating the invariant that totalUnits == sum(lender credits) == withdrawable + outstanding debt, causing scoped impact: accounting desync where lenders can withdraw more than deposited? Proof idea: rebase token upward between takes, assert sum(credits) != midnight.balanceOf(loanToken) - claimableSettlementFee.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (payer path)] Can an unprivileged taker use a rebasing (negative rebase) loan token under the precondition that the token's balances decrease between take() calls, trigger a sequence of take() then withdraw() so that position.credit was set during take but the actual token balance shrank via rebase, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= actual loan token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where later lenders cannot withdraw? Proof idea: rebase token downward after take, attempt withdraw, assert revert or shortfall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a rebasing collateral token under the precondition that the token's balance of Midnight increases via positive rebase after supplyCollateral(), trigger withdrawCollateral(assets + rebaseGain) so that position.collateral[i] underflows or the health check uses stale collateral accounting, violating the invariant that position.collateral[i] == actual tokens held for that borrower, causing scoped impact: borrower withdraws more collateral than deposited, draining other users' collateral? Proof idea: supply collateral, rebase up, attempt to withdraw more than deposited, assert revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender exploit a rebasing collateral token used as loanToken under the precondition that Midnight holds rebased tokens, trigger withdraw() for more units than originally credited, violating the invariant that position.credit >= units withdrawn, causing scoped impact: unauthorized withdrawal of other lenders' funds? Proof idea: fuzz rebase factor, assert sum of all withdrawals <= initial deposits.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use an ERC777-compatible loan token with a tokensReceived hook under the precondition that the hook reenters Midnight during safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets), trigger take() so that the reentrant call observes partially updated state (credit/debt already updated, tokens not yet received), violating the invariant that token receipt and accounting update are atomic, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts credit without paying? Proof idea: implement ERC777 hook that calls withdraw() during onBuy callback, assert credit extracted without full payment.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower use an ERC777-compatible loan token under the precondition that the tokensReceived hook fires during safeTransferFrom in repay(), trigger repay() so that the hook reenters repay() or withdraw() while position.debt has already been reduced but tokens not yet received, violating the invariant that debt reduction and token receipt are atomic, causing scoped impact: borrower reduces debt twice without paying twice? Proof idea: ERC777 hook reenters repay() during onRepay callback, assert debt reduced by 2*units but only units paid.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use an ERC777-compatible collateral token under the precondition that the tokensToSend hook fires during safeTransferFrom in supplyCollateral(), trigger supplyCollateral() so that the hook reenters withdrawCollateral() before collateral is actually received, violating the invariant that collateral accounting only increases after tokens are received, causing scoped impact: borrower withdraws collateral that was never deposited? Proof idea: ERC777 tokensToSend hook calls withdrawCollateral() during supplyCollateral(), assert net collateral balance is negative.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender use an ERC777-compatible loan token under the precondition that the tokensReceived hook fires during safeTransfer in withdraw(), trigger withdraw() so that the hook reenters withdraw() again before the first call's state is finalized, violating the invariant that position.credit is decremented before token transfer, causing scoped impact: double withdrawal of credit? Proof idea: note that credit is decremented before safeTransfer in withdraw(); verify reentrancy cannot exploit the ordering by checking if hook can call withdraw() again with remaining credit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use a token that returns false on transferFrom (non-reverting failure) under the precondition that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom checks returndata.length == 0 || abi.decode(returndata, (bool)), trigger take() with a token that returns false, causing safeTransferFrom to revert with TransferFromReturnedFalse(), violating no accounting invariant but verifying that the check is correctly applied and cannot be bypassed by a token returning a non-bool ABI-encoded value? Proof idea: deploy token returning abi.encode(false), assert take() reverts; deploy token returning abi.encode(uint256(0)), assert behavior.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged market creator use a token with no deployed code (EOA address or self-destructed contract) as loanToken under the precondition that SafeTransferLib checks token.code.length > 0, trigger take() so that the NoCode() revert fires, but verify whether touchMarket() creates the market before the code check, allowing a market to be created with a non-contract token that later gets code deployed, violating the invariant that loanToken must always be a valid ERC20 contract? Proof idea: create market with EOA as loanToken (if touchMarket allows it), deploy code to that address later, execute take(), assert accounting correctness.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use a token whose transferFrom silently transfers less than the requested amount (partial transfer, no revert, returns true) under the precondition that SafeTransferLib does not verify the actual amount transferred, trigger take() so that buyerAssets - sellerAssets is credited to claimableSettlementFee and sellerAssets is credited to the seller, but the contract received less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency? Proof idea: deploy token that transfers min(requested, cap) silently, execute take(), assert balance shortfall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker use a token with non-standard decimals (e.g., 6 decimals) as loanToken under the precondition that the protocol uses WAD (1e18) for all price/unit math and ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE (1e36) for oracle prices, trigger take() so that buyerAssets and sellerAssets are computed in WAD-scaled units but the actual token transfer uses 6-decimal amounts, violating the invariant that token transfer amounts match accounting unit amounts, causing scoped impact: massive over- or under-payment depending on decimal mismatch? Proof idea: fuzz token decimals in [6, 8, 18, 27], assert buyerAssets == actual tokens transferred.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower use a collateral token with non-standard decimals under the precondition that the oracle price is scaled to ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE (1e36) and maxDebt = collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD, trigger supplyCollateral(assets) and then borrow, so that if collateral token has 6 decimals but oracle price is calibrated for 18 decimals, maxDebt is computed incorrectly, violating the invariant that maxDebt correctly reflects the USD value of collateral, causing scoped impact: undercollateralized borrowing or healthy positions incorrectly liquidated? Proof idea: fuzz collateral decimals and oracle price scale, assert maxDebt == expected USD value.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / flashLoan] Can an unprivileged flash loan user use a fee-on-transfer token under the precondition that flashLoan() sends assets[i] to callback via safeTransfer and then pulls assets[i] back via safeTransferFrom, trigger flashLoan() so that callback receives (assets[i] - fee) but must repay assets[i], causing the repayment safeTransferFrom to pull assets[i] from callback but Midnight only receives (assets[i] - fee), violating the invariant that Midnight's balance is unchanged after a flash loan, causing scoped impact: protocol balance drained by fee amount per flash loan? Proof idea: flash loan with fee-on-transfer token, assert midnight.balanceOf(token) decreases by fee after each flash loan.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Critical Direct loss of user funds through incorrect credit, debt, collateral, withdrawable, or fee accounting'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take (payer is callback)] Can an unprivileged maker use a callback contract as payer under the precondition that payer = buyerCallback when buyerCallback != address(0), trigger take() so that the IBuyCallback.onBuy() is called before safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets), and the callback uses a fee-on-transfer token to pay, violating the invariant that the protocol receives exactly buyerAssets - sellerAssets, causing scoped impact: settlement fee underfunded, protocol insolvency? Proof idea: implement onBuy callback with fee-on-transfer token payment, assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance.
